name: Benissa Ecological Coastal Path
name_original: Ruta costera al norte de Cal
description: Hike the moderate 6.3-mile Coastal route to the north of Cal, enjoying beaches and promenades with 311 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Benissa offers diverse landscapes, blending rugged mountains with stunning coastlines. The region is characterized by dramatic mountain ranges like the Sierra de Bèrnia and serene valleys, alongside a picturesque Mediterranean coastline featuring secluded coves and cliffs. Pine forests and rural countryside also contribute to the varied terrain. This geographical mix provides a wide array of trails for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Benissa The most popular hiking route is Benissa Ecological Coastal Path, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This scenic path winds along the coastline, offering spectacular sea views and passing through various coves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Bèrnia Loop Trail, a moderate 1.8 miles (2.9 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the Costa Blanca and includes a unique natural tunnel through the mountainside. Local hikers also love the Riberers' Route Trail (PR-CV 388), a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through varied countryside with stunning views over the Marina Alta, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Benissa is defined by coastal paths, mountainous terrain, and tranquil rural routes through pine forests and valleys. The network offers options for easy family strolls as well as more challenging mountain treks. The routes in Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 560 reviews. More than 3,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Benissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Benissa?
A: Benissa offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with nearly 100 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to all levels, from easy coastal strolls to more challenging mountain treks.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking trails in Benissa?
A: Yes, Benissa has many easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351369717/from-les-bassetes-to-buenavista-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Les Bassetes to Buenavista — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.5 km path. The Benissa Ecological Coastal Path is also largely flat and offers stunning sea views, making it enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What are the best trails for scenic views in Benissa?
A: For breathtaking panoramas, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351360440/sierra-de-bernia-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Bèrnia Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is highly recommended, offering expansive views of the Costa Blanca. The Benissa Ecological Coastal Path also provides spectacular sea views along its entire length, passing by picturesque coves and cliffs.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Benissa?
A: Yes, Benissa features several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351369717/from-les-bassetes-to-buenavista-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Les Bassetes to Buenavista — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351360440/sierra-de-bernia-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Bèrnia Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate challenge with incredible mountain scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Benissa?
A: The best time for hiking in Benissa is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is pleasantly mild, making it ideal for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. Summers can be very hot, especially for longer or more exposed routes.

Q: Are there any challenging hiking trails for experienced hikers in Benissa?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351359467/riberers-route-trail-pr-cv-388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riberers' Route Trail (PR-CV 388)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15 km trail that leads through varied countryside with stunning views over the Marina Alta. The Sierra de Bèrnia also offers more demanding sections for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on Benissa's trails?
A: Benissa's trails showcase diverse natural beauty. You can expect dramatic mountain ranges like the Sierra de Bèrnia, with its unique 'Forat' tunnel, and serene valleys. Along the coast, you'll find secluded coves such as Cala de la Llobella, Cala Pinets, Cala Baladrar, and Cala Fustera, as well as impressive cliffs and views of the Peñón de Ifach. Inland routes often pass through pine forests and rural landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Benissa?
A: The hiking trails in Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 560 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's unique blend of stunning coastal panoramas and rugged mountain landscapes, as well as the variety of trails suitable for all abilities.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Benissa's surroundings offer several historical attractions. You can explore various castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell d'Aixa Ruins and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351336820/quisi-mill-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quisi Mill Route&lt;/a&gt; also leads to a restored 15th-century water mill, offering a glimpse into local history.

Q: Are Benissa's hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: Many of Benissa's natural trails and rural paths are suitable for dogs, especially those away from crowded areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in protected natural areas or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Benissa?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular hiking areas and coastal access points in Benissa, such as those along the Benissa Ecological Coastal Path or near the coves, offer designated parking areas. For inland routes, parking might be available in nearby villages or designated rural spots.

Q: Can I reach Benissa's hiking trails using public transport?
A: Benissa town is accessible by public transport, including bus services connecting it to other towns on the Costa Blanca. From the town center, some trails are within walking distance, while others, particularly those in the mountains or more remote rural areas, may require a short taxi ride or personal vehicle to reach the trailhead.
tips: Cala La Fustera is the main beach of Benissa. With a length of 110 meters and a width of about 30 meters, this corner of the Costa Blanca offers us fine sand and a landscape of great beauty.

By means of a ramp located on the pedestrian promenade, we access this curious cove whose rocks accumulate remains of underwater vegetation that guarantee us healthy seabeds full of life. Next to the Calalga park, with fine sand and boulders.

The promenade in Calpe is a lovely walk all the way, with numerous bars and restaurants along the way, with lovely sand and gentle waves lapping on the shore and plenty of resting spots, paradise!
The promenade in Calpe was very well maintained, allowing for a pleasant walk along the promenade on a mostly flat surface.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this coastal route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it offers beautiful coastal views, it's suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this hike is approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes, covering a distance of about 10.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route features several beautiful coastal spots. You'll pass by La Fustera Cove, Calalga Beach, and the scenic La Fossa Beach Promenade (Paseo Marítimo de la Fossa).

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, the trail is generally suitable for those with some hiking experience. While not overly strenuous, it's a good idea for beginners or families with young children to assess their fitness levels before embarking on the full route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this coastal route?
faqs.answer: Coastal routes are often best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally pleasant. Summer can be very warm, while winter might bring more unpredictable weather.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails, including the Paseo ecologico de Besnissa (covering about 45% of the route), Carrer de la Perera (10%), and parts of Galeria Voramar.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this coastal route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on many coastal paths, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific rules for beaches and promenades can vary locally. It's advisable to check local signage, particularly around the beach areas like La Fustera Cove and Calalga Beach.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access this coastal route or the public beaches and promenades along it.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of a coastal path. You can expect paved sections along promenades, sandy stretches near beaches, and potentially some unpaved or uneven sections as you navigate coves and natural areas.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As a coastal route passing through populated areas, there are usually public parking options available in the vicinity of the starting points, especially near the beaches and promenades. Look for designated parking areas in Calpe or nearby towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate coastal hike, it's recommended to bring comfortable walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a camera for the scenic views. Depending on the season, a light jacket might also be useful.

waypoints.name: La Fossa Beach Promenade (Paseo Marítimo de la Fossa)
